Trap Bar Deadlift.alt: Lifter completes a trap-style deadlift in competition
Trap Bar Deadlift.movementDescription: Step inside the frame with feet about hip-width and brace your trunk. Hinge from the hips, grip the handles, flatten the back, then drive the floor away and stand tall—hips and knees finishing together. Lower with the same hinge pattern to reset each rep.
Trap Bar Deadlift.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • multiple authors / CC BY-SA (see file page)

Front Squat.alt: Diagram showing the barbell front squat posture
Front Squat.movementDescription: Rack the bar on the fronts of your shoulders with elbows lifted and torso stacked. Brace, sit the hips between the heels while keeping ribs down, then drive evenly through mid-foot until you stand. Keep the elbows high throughout so the bar cannot roll forward.

Safety Bar Box Squat.alt: Athlete squatting with a heavily loaded barbell
Safety Bar Box Squat.movementDescription: Set a box slightly above parallel and sit the speciality bar onto your upper back—hands on the horns, torso braced. Break at hips and knees together, pause on the box without relaxing, then stand by driving hips under the bar. Adjust box height so depth is repeatable every rep.
Safety Bar Box Squat.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • US military work / PD

Heavy Sled Push.alt: Athletes sprinting whilst pushing sleds indoors
Heavy Sled Push.movementDescription: Set your shoulders above the uprights, elbows locked in, hips low enough to chase a stiff trunk. Drive short, choppy strides through straight arms—stay over the sled so force goes forward, not up. Finish the prescribed distance without standing upright or letting the lumbar round.
Heavy Sled Push.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • US Army photographers / PD

Scrum Machine Isometrics.alt: Rugby union scrum contested by two packs
Scrum Machine Isometrics.movementDescription: Pack into machine or partner setup at scrum angle with full foot contact and locked shoulders. Breathe in low, tighten trunk, then build pressure as if driving through the sled—typically 5–10 s per effort—with no collapsing at the hips. Reset calmly between repetitions.

Zercher Carry.alt: Animation of a lifter performing a Zercher-style squat
Zercher Carry.movementDescription: Cradle the bar in the crook of the elbows and interlace fingers or hold a neutral grip. Pack the lats, stand tall, and walk with short steps while keeping ribs stacked over pelvis. Do not let the bar drift forward or the upper back round during the carry.

Heavy Farmer Carry.alt: Strongman training event with heavy farmer carry implements
Heavy Farmer Carry.movementDescription: Lift both implements together, pack shoulders down, and begin marching with minimal lateral sway. Breathe behind the brace, keep head tall, and put each foot down quietly to avoid leaking energy sideways. Walk the set distance or time without setting the weights down.

Copenhagen Plank.alt: Person demonstrates a modified side plank on the elbow
Copenhagen Plank.movementDescription: Side-on, support on the elbow with the top leg rested on a bench or box whilst the bottom leg hangs free. Elevate hips in a straight line, squeeze adductors, and breathe without dropping the torso. Swap sides equally and control the eccentric when lowering.
Copenhagen Plank.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • National Cancer Institute / PD

Weighted Deadbug.alt: Instructional collage of abdominal training positions
Weighted Deadbug.movementDescription: Lie supine, ribs pinned to floor, thighs near 90° with calves parallel. Brace as you lengthen one limb at a time or use a light plate held over the sternum—the low back stays fixed to the floor. Move through small ranges rather than swinging the torso.
Weighted Deadbug.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • National Cancer Institute / PD

Landmine Rotation.alt: Soldier performing an explosive ballistic throw forwards
Landmine Rotation.movementDescription: Wedge one end of a barbell in a landmine with a clasped grip near the sternum. Root the feet, hinge slightly, rotate the torso on a stiff mid-line, then control back to centre. Alternate sides evenly and chase smooth arcs rather than yanking through the wrists.
Landmine Rotation.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • US military work / PD

Wrestler Bridges.alt: Anatomy illustration highlighting muscles of the back
Wrestler Bridges.movementDescription: Only after thorough coaching: start from mats, bridging onto the forehead or crown with hands spotting for stability. Peel the spine sequentially, shorten range if there is dizziness, then lower with control—never jerk into extension. Prefer low volume sets with perfect technique.
Wrestler Bridges.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • OpenStax Anatomy and Physiology / CC BY 3.0

Banded Neck Isometrics.alt: Athlete demonstrating a plank hold with neutral spine
Banded Neck Isometrics.movementDescription: Fix a moderate band anchor at head height—press the forehead lightly into band flexion without nodding sharply, then repeat extension and each lateral tilt for holds of 15–25 s. Keep jaw relaxed and shoulders squared; chase pressure, not amplitude.
Banded Neck Isometrics.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • Simon Speed / CC BY 3.0

Shrugs and Trap 3 Raises.alt: Hex-shaped trap bar on the gym floor
Shrugs and Trap 3 Raises.movementDescription: Grip dumbbells or a trap bar—elevate shoulders straight up whilst arms stay loose, squeeze one second at the peak, then lower deliberately. Trap-3 raises add a hinge: lean torso forward slightly and raise angled to ear height focusing on posterior shoulder. Keep reps strict and shrug vertically only.

Seal Rows.movementDescription: Lie chest-down on an elevated padded bench holding dumbbells perpendicular to torso. Brace through glutes so ribcage cannot bounce, pull elbows towards hips without flaring wrists, squeeze mid-back briefly, then lower under tension. Mimic the torso angle you'd use pressing into a bind.

Hang Cleans.movementDescription: Start with the bar just above knees, hips back yet shoulders over bar. Brace, jump vertically by extending ankles, knees and hips simultaneously, shrug and pull elbows high and outside, flip under to catch solidly across the fronts of the thighs. Ride the catch with soft knees—not a premature press-out.
Hang Cleans.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • Kmitu / CC BY-SA 3.0 PL

Box Jumps.alt: Sailor performs a plyometric jump onto plyo boxes aboard ship
Box Jumps.movementDescription: Stand close but not cramped to the plyo-box, hinge slightly and swing arms if allowed, then explode so hips extend before feet leave floor. Aim to land softly with knees tracking over toes and hips higher than squat depth. Step down calmly—jumping down wastes joints—then reset posture before the next repetition.
Box Jumps.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • US Navy photographers / PD

Broad Jumps.alt: Vintage athlete midway through standing long jump flight
Broad Jumps.movementDescription: Start tall with ankles, knees and hips stacked, hinge quickly and punch arms forward simultaneously with take-off so power travels horizontally. Aim to cushion each landing absorbing through hips—never collapsing into valgus—or mark distance on turf for feedback. Restrict volume once bar speed fades.
Broad Jumps.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons • Bain News Service collection / PD

Medicine Ball Chest Drives.alt: Ten-pound medicine ball resting upright on turf
Medicine Ball Chest Drives.movementDescription: Hold the medicine ball sternum-high with elbows under wrists, widen stance for balance, brace then violently pulse the torso forward—as if ripping through tackles—until the ball sails at chest height. Catch or retrieve smoothly, minimise lumbar hyper-extension, repeat with crisp intent across low rep sets.
Medicine Ball Chest Drives.sourceNote: Wikimedia Commons (licence noted on file page)
